title: boechera lyallii
intro: Boechera lyallii, also known as Lyall's rockcress, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the Brassicaceae family. It is a rare and endangered species native to the Pacific Northwest region of North America, known for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to treat respiratory issues and skin conditions. The plant's leaves and flowers contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, providing health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting immune function. Traditionally, Boechera lyallii is prepared into herbal teas, salves, and infusions, which can be used to relieve coughs, soothe skin irritations, and promote overall well-being.
